family: Fries-, Photo-Fries, and Anionic Ortho-Fries Rearrangement
rationale: The Lewis acid-catalyzed Fries rearrangement of phenyl acetate to 2-hydroxyacetophenone is the simplest and most frequently cited textbook example of this reaction family. It clearly demonstrates the core transformation: the intramolecular rearrangement of an acyl group from a phenolic oxygen to an ortho position on the aromatic ring.
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses the simplest possible aromatic system (phenyl) and acyl group (acetyl) to avoid overlap with more complex named reactions involving substituted arenes or specific functional groups. It avoids motifs common in other rearrangements (e.g., Claisen, which involves allyl groups) or electrophilic aromatic substitutions (e.g., Friedel-Crafts acylation, which is intermolecular).
